  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Unblocked soffits create an open path for fire spread between the wall cavity and the ceiling/attic space.",
    "Missing fireblocking at the connection between the vertical wall and horizontal soffit space."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install approved fireblocking materials, such as 2-inch nominal lumber, two layers of 1-inch nominal lumber, or 3/4-inch plywood/OSB, to seal the connection between the soffit and the wall cavity.",
    "Ensure all joints and penetrations in the fireblocking are tightly sealed with approved firestop sealant."
  ],
  "rationale": "Per IRC Section R302.11, fireblocking must be provided in concealed spaces of stud walls and partitions, including at connections between horizontal and vertical spaces such as soffits, drop ceilings, and cove ceilings. Unblocked soffits allow fire to bypass wall assemblies and rapidly spread into the floor or roof structure above, presenting a high safety risk.",
  "references": [
    "IRC R302.11",
    "IRC R302.11.1"
  ]
}
